message:
feat(atc): Tracks 13-15 — VCG mechanism design, queueing theory, PID controller

Track 13: VCG-inspired priority auction for fair conflict resolution.
  Truthful reporting is the dominant strategy; highest-externality
  agent yields first.

Track 14: Pollaczek-Khinchine and Kingman queueing formulas for
  provable wait-time estimates. Little's Law consistency checker
  for model validation.

Track 15: PID regret controller with anti-windup, safety bounds
  [0.1×, 10×], and operator reset. Drives loss matrix toward values
  that minimize observed regret.

11 new tests, 101 total passing. All 15 implementation tracks complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
